answeredQuestions.initiative-name: Scout Meal Planner MVP Launch — pilot with a single scout troop, potential expansion to other troops
answeredQuestions.business-problem: Meal planning takes too long in meetings; scouts record things inconsistently; communication breakdowns between scouts and leaders result in incorrect food purchases, wrong equipment, and less time for other activities
answeredQuestions.initiative-type: New product pilot with potential multi-troop expansion
answeredQuestions.primary-stakeholders: Scoutmaster (sponsor), Assistant Scoutmaster, SPL (youth leader), Scouts (primary users), Parents of scouts (secondary users)
answeredQuestions.meeting-workflow: SPL-led meeting: logistics briefing → meal list with parameters → brainstorm and vote → patrol assignment → detailed patrol planning → handoff to Scoutmaster → transcribe and send to parents → shop → pack → post-trip retro
answeredQuestions.recording-methods: Inconsistent: paper, phones, memory. Scoutmaster manually transcribes. No reusable recipe record.
answeredQuestions.shopping-and-packing: Shopping list divided by meal, assigned to individual scouts. Scouts buy and bring to departure point.
answeredQuestions.must-have-for-launch: Full meal planning workflow for a trip plus recipe library for reuse
answeredQuestions.can-wait: Multi-troop support, cross-troop recipe sharing, advanced filters/favorites/feedback
answeredQuestions.timeline: Prototype by May 2026, live by September 2026
answeredQuestions.meeting-time-baseline: 30-60+ minutes per meeting for meal planning
answeredQuestions.shopping-equipment-errors: Most trips have issues — also common: recipe steps incomplete or not brought on trip
answeredQuestions.troop-size: ~25 scouts, ~10 adult leaders, occasional parents/guests. Trip groups 8-20+ people.
answeredQuestions.scout-adoption: Ages 12-18, generally comfortable with web apps. Mobile-first web required. Native app is stretch goal.
answeredQuestions.parent-access: Shareable link is sufficient — no parent login needed for MVP
answeredQuestions.biggest-risk: Scouting America technology platform compliance for youth-facing app. Need PII handling, communication compliance, content moderation (Azure Content Safety or similar).
answeredQuestions.patrol-assignment: Not in MVP — one scout (patrol leader) records information. No patrol management in-app.
answeredQuestions.brainstorming-voting: Not in MVP — SPL records decided meals after in-person discussion.
answeredQuestions.shopping-assignment: App should email shopping list. No in-app assignment to individual scout shoppers.
answeredQuestions.recipe-on-trip: Recipes must be printable. Offline access is a future nice-to-have.

referencesProcessed.keyFindings: Detailed product features: event/meal scheduling, recipe management with variations, dynamic scaling, equipment tracking, shopping list generation, custom notes, scout feedback system. Design direction, component selection, and UX patterns defined.

nextActions: Review and validate full BRD for completeness
